title: HikariCP pool exhaustion under concurrent inventory lock contention
description: When multiple checkout requests attempt to acquire a ShareLock on the same inventory row simultaneously, transactions hold DB connections for 10–30s instead of the typical 200ms. With pool_size ≤ 5, all connections are consumed within seconds and subsequent requests time out at the connection-acquire layer (SocketTimeoutException after 30s). Observed when pool_size < (peak_concurrent_checkouts × avg_transaction_hold_time / 1000).
root_cause: Inventory row-level locking during order creation serializes concurrent checkouts. HikariCP pool too small to absorb lock wait queue.
workaround: Set pool_size ≥ 20 for production workloads. Alternatively, implement optimistic locking on inventory to reduce hold time.
resolution: Add pool_size ≥ 20 as a hard minimum in production config. Gate config changes behind a load-test validation step.

title: Payment gateway upstream timeout cascades into checkout 504s
description: When the payment gateway is slow (>5s response), the checkout service holds its DB connection open for the full transaction duration while waiting. Under moderate concurrency this exhausts the pool. Resolved by separating payment confirmation into an async step so DB connection is released before gateway call.
root_cause: Synchronous payment gateway call inside DB transaction held connection open during network wait.
workaround: Release DB connection before calling payment gateway.
resolution: Refactored checkout flow in PR-344: DB commit → release connection → call payment gateway → async confirmation.

title: Missing pool_size validation gate in deployment pipeline
description: The deployment pipeline does not validate DB connection pool configuration changes against a minimum safe threshold before promoting to production. Config-only changes skip load testing. This allowed pool_size to be reduced from 20→5 without a guard rail.
root_cause: No config approval gate for database pool parameters in the GitHub Actions deploy workflow.
workaround: Manual peer review of config diffs — fragile and not enforced.
resolution: Add policy-as-code check: reject deploys where pool_size < 10 without explicit override. Ticket: INFRA-5521.

title: Redis cache miss storm on checkout-service restart
description: On pod restart, all in-memory cart caches are cold. If multiple pods restart simultaneously, every request hits Postgres directly causing a brief query spike. Not related to connection pool sizing.
root_cause: No cache warm-up on startup; cold start under traffic causes DB query burst.
workaround: Rolling restarts with 60s delay between pods.
resolution: Added startup cache pre-warming in PR-401.

title: Autovacuum lag causes index bloat under high UPDATE workload
description: High UPDATE rate on the orders and inventory tables during incident periods outpaces autovacuum, causing dead tuple accumulation and table bloat. Query performance degrades 20–40% under bloat. Observed as a secondary effect during pool exhaustion incidents when aborted transactions leave dead tuples.
root_cause: Default autovacuum thresholds too conservative for high-churn tables.
resolution: Tuned autovacuum parameters per-table in Postgres config (INFRA-5490). Monitoring bloat with pg_stat_user_tables.
